channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Giants bench QB Daniel Jones and will start Tommy DeVito vs. the Bucs, AP source says
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — The New York Giants are benching struggling quarterback Daniel Jones and will start fan favorite Tommy DeVito against the Tampa Bay Buccaneers this week in an attempt to break a five-game losing streak, according to a person with knowledge of the decision.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: James Harden breaks tie with Ray Allen, moves to No. 2 on NBA's career 3-point list
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden now stands alone at No. 2 in 3-pointers. The Los Angeles Clippers guard connected on the 2,974th 3-pointer of his career Sunday night and broke a tie with Basketball Hall of Famer Ray Allen for the No. 2 spot on the NBA’s list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Kris Byrant's Lamborghini Huracan stolen from Colorado home
item.description: Kris Bryant's Lamborghini Huracan was stolen by a multi-state vehicle theft ring, but was recovered by police in Las Vegas after they traced the stolen vehicle to a home in the city.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Richardson's late TD run leads Colts to a 28-27 victory over Rodgers and the Jets
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — Anthony Richardson was back as the Indianapolis Colts' starting quarterback — and played as though he intends to never leave the huddle again. The No.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mountain West Conference determines there's insufficient evidence in volleyball Title IX complaint
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The Mountain West Conference has concluded there was insufficient evidence to determine misconduct between women's volleyball players from the Colorado State and San Jose State programs ahead of a match last month after a coach filed a Title IX complaint alleging the student-ath
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utah HC falls at home to Vegas, 4-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - William Karlsson scored the go-ahead goal with 1:18 remaining, and the Vegas Golden Knights rallied for a 4-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Delta Center on Friday. “We stuck with it and eventually we got rewarded,” Karlsson said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rodgers plans to return to Jets for 2025 season
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Did you see the parade? The celebration? Missed it? So did I. When New York Jets quarterback Aaron Rodgers said he has every intention of playing in 2025, the announcement was met with a groan.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: Mike Tyson slapped Jake Paul at their final weigh-in. Here's why Tyson claims he did it
item.description: A meeting between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul briefly devolved into chaos on Thursday after Tyson slapped Paul across the face during the final weigh-in before their highly anticipated fight on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Clippers write love letter to Los Angeles with new City Edition uniforms
item.description: With the NBA now in full swing and the brand-new Intuit Dome open for business, the Los Angeles Clippers have unveiled their City Edition uniforms for the new season. The new look, which the team says symbolizes the story and work of L.A.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Grand Prix of Long Beach sold ahead of iconic race's 50th edition
item.description: The Grand Prix of Long Long Beach, the longest-running major street circuit race in North America, will have a new owner heading into the 50th edition of the event next year. Penske Entertainment announced its purchase of Grand Prix Association of Long Beach, LLC, from longtime owner Gerald R.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Bears release OL Nate Davis
item.description: CHICAGO — The Nate Davis era is over at Halas Hall after the Bears cut him Wednesday. The lineman had a tough time finding the field despite being paid over $10 million a year.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Sky introduce new head coach Tyler Marsh
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — A little more than a month after former head coach Teresa Weatherspoon was unceremoniously relieved of her duties, the Chicago Sky introduced their new head coach to the Windy City Tuesday. Tyler Marsh, who was hired on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Mitchell scores 36 as the Cavs beat the Bulls 119-113 for 12th straight win
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored a season-high 36 points, and the Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Chicago Bulls 119-113 on Monday night to become the eighth NBA team to begin a season with 12 straight wins.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lightning hosts 7th annual Coops Catch for Kids
item.description: Tampa Bay Lightning Head Coach Jon Cooper hosted the 7th annual Coop’s Catch for Kids, presented by Heritage Insurance, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC
